name: Log Aggregation and Streaming
description: Stream and aggregate logs from pods, deployments, and multiple containers using kubectl and label selectors.

steps.title: Stream logs from a pod
steps.description: Follow log output from a specific pod in real time.

steps.title: Stream logs from all pods in a deployment
steps.description: Follow logs from every pod matching a label selector.
steps.tip: The --max-log-requests flag limits concurrent log streams. Increase it for deployments with many replicas.

steps.title: View logs from a previous container instance
steps.description: See logs from a container that crashed and was restarted.
steps.tip: This is essential for debugging CrashLoopBackOff pods since the current container may not have useful output yet.

steps.title: Filter logs by time range
steps.description: Show logs from a specific time window.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Log Aggregation and Streaming
set -euo pipefail

SELECTOR=${1:?"Usage: $0 <label-selector> [namespace]"}
NS=${2:-"default"}

echo "=== Pods matching selector: $SELECTOR ==="
kubectl get pods -n "$NS" -l "$SELECTOR" -o wide

echo ""
echo "=== Recent logs (last 50 lines per pod) ==="
kubectl logs -n "$NS" -l "$SELECTOR" --all-containers --tail=50 --timestamps

echo ""
echo "=== Streaming Commands ==="
echo "Follow all:    kubectl logs -f -n $NS -l $SELECTOR --all-containers"
echo "Since 1h:      kubectl logs -n $NS -l $SELECTOR --since=1h"
echo "Previous:      kubectl logs -n $NS <pod-name> --previous"

faq.question: How do I view logs from init containers?
faq.answer: Use 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -c <init-container-name>'. List init containers with 'kubectl get pod <pod-name> -o jsonpath={.spec.initContainers[*].name}'.

faq.question: Is there a way to search/grep through logs?
faq.answer: Pipe kubectl logs to grep: 'kubectl logs <pod-name> | grep ERROR'. For production, consider a log aggregation system like Loki, Elasticsearch, or CloudWatch Logs.
